explanation: The property states that globally, it must always be the case that if the system is in PSV mode AND the breathing time is greater than or equal to the allowable apnea lag time, then apnea is detected. This captures the requirement that the system checks for and detects apnea when its defining condition is met while in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode and the condition for apnea (patient not taking a new breath within the allowable apnea lag time) is met, then apnea is detected by the system.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PSV mode and the condition for apnea (patient not taking a new breath within the allowable apnea lag time) is met, then the system must detect apnea. This is captured by the universal implication: if (PSV mode AND breathing time >= apnea lag time), then apnea is detected.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode and the condition for apnea (patient not taking a new breath within the allowable apnea lag time) is met, then apnea must eventually be detected. This captures the system's responsibility to monitor for and signal apnea when its defined conditions are present in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Support Ventilation mode (PSVMode) and the breathing time is greater than or equal to the allowable apnea lag time (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), then apnea is detected (apnea). This directly captures the condition under which apnea occurs specifically when the system is in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp1) and the breathing time is greater than or equal to the allowed apnea lag time (bool_exp2), then apnea (bool_exp3) is detected. This directly captures the requirement that apnea occurs when a patient does not take a new breath within the allowable apnea lag time while in PSV mode.

This directly captures the requirement that apnea occurs when the specified conditions are met while in PSV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV mode (bool_exp1) and the condition for apnea (a patient not taking a new breath within the allowable apnea lag time, represented by bool_exp2) is met, then apnea should be detected (bool_exp3). This directly captures the requirement that in PSV mode, the system shall check for and detect apnea when the specified condition occurs.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode AND the breath timer is greater than or equal to the allowable apnea lag time (which means a patient has not taken a new breath within the allowed time), THEN apnea is detected. This directly translates the natural language requirement which defines when apnea occurs in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode and a patient does not take a new breath within the allowable apnea lag time, then apnea must be detected. This is a safety requirement ensuring that apnea is recognized when its conditions are met within the specified operating mode.
